basics.name: Aleister Vaillant
basics.label: ML/CV Engineer
basics.summary: Six years of noticing that a specific tool was missing and building it. CV model trained locally and deployed to a Raspberry Pi. Asset tracking for 10,000+ hardware units. Autonomous vehicle fault triage.

work.name: StoryverseNYC
work.summary: CV systems for GÖSPEL, an immersive theater production.
highlights: Wrote the CV pipeline from scratch: scripted Blender to generate synthetic training data, parallelized GPU rendering across workers, fine-tuned YOLO, added Kalman filter tracking
Quantized models to TFLite INT8 and ONNX. Runs real-time on a Raspberry Pi with Arduino output control
Benchmarking system replays venue video against a mocked state machine to compare model configurations
Early work on LLM agent architecture with RFID hardware for audience-responsive experiences

work.name: X, the moonshot factory (Chorus)
work.summary: Python test infrastructure for prototype GPS/sensor hardware. The firmware team hired me out of ops because we'd worked well together.
highlights: Flash memory reliability test framework. Cut manual verification by 60%
BLE connectivity studies for the Product team
Proof-of-concept applications to derisk hardware approaches before committing resources

work.name: X, the moonshot factory (Chorus)
work.summary: Started in operations shipping boxes. No inventory database existed, just a provisioning spreadsheet. I built one.
highlights: Asset management system: labeled boxes, mapped devices to boxes, linked test results to device IDs. Shipping went from scanning every unit to filtering by box name. 10,000+ units tracked
Python pipeline comparing sensor measurements against ground truth. Automated report generation improved QA throughput 30%
ETL pipeline integrating the asset system into a relational database
Handled device shipping logistics for external partners

work.name: Waymo
work.summary: Triaged autonomous vehicle faults under case-volume quotas. Learned the full system architecture to make the call: hardware issue, misclassified object, or hallucination.
highlights: Reverse-engineered parts of the playback log schema. Used it to build a hard brake report
Got into the C++ source of the internal log viewer (XView), compiled locally, added hotkeys and probability displays along behavior paths

projects.name: A Long Day in Hell
projects.description: Browser-based roguelike built in 7 days. Procedurally generated infinite library where every book is deterministically derived from coordinates via base-95 bijection. ECS social simulation with 120 NPCs.
highlights: 1200+ tests, 439 commits, single self-contained HTML file
Coordinate algebra guaranteeing player's book is within navigable space by construction

projects.name: Paranoia Agent
projects.description: Adversarial code reviewer implementing Verification-Driven Development. Reviews with escalating hostility until forced to hallucinate problems — that's the exit condition.

projects.name: Factorio Working Group
projects.description: First-author research applying Path of Destruction to factory layout generation. 21-channel binary matrix representation; best model achieved 99.82% validation accuracy.

projects.name: Petrock
projects.description: Multimodal AI on Raspberry Pi: real-time CV inference with LLM-driven personality on edge hardware.
